events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need assess PPE selection residential job, observed respirator wrong cartridge color (ID). Work rough-complete. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Wrong cartridge color means respirator not appropriate for hazard, non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk? Respirator wrong cartridge can be high if exposure to hazardous particles/gases; but residential context maybe medium/high. OSHA PPE selection requires proper selection per hazard. NFPA 70E maybe arc flash PPE not relevant. Wrong cartridge color indicates incorrect filter for contaminant; could lead to exposure. Risk high. component_conditions: installed-defective? It's PPE not installed component, but categories allowed include installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. Use non-compliant, installed-defective maybe not. The scenario says component condition categories may apply: installed-defective, non-compliant. Since PPE is equipment, wrong cartridge color is non-compliant. Could also be installed-defective? Maybe not installed. Use ["non-compliant"].

percent_complete rough-complete maybe 70. value 0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, order [], workable []. findings: "Respirator cartridge color/ID does not match the contaminant/hazard present; incorrect respiratory protection selected." actions: "Replace cartridge with correct type per manufacturer and hazard assessment; verify fit test and respirator program." rationale: "OSHA 1910.134 requires respirators selected based on respiratory hazard; wrong cartridge color indicates improper selection and will not provide required protection, making work non-compliant." references: ["OSHA 1910.134", "NFPA 70E 130.7(C)", "OSHA 1910 Subpart I"]. Need ensure valid JSON. No prose outside.
